A weekly audio discussion with Dr. Ivan Misner, the Founder and Chief Visionary Officer of BNI, the world's largest business networking organization.

Special Episode 3: We Are All in This Together

April 1, 2020 by Sallie Goetsch 20 Comments

Podcast: Download

Subscribe: Apple Podcasts | RSS | Subscription Options

Print This Episode
This entry is part 2 of 4 in the series Special Episodes

Synopsis

In this third special episode, Dr. Misner shares comments from BNI members around the world about the new BNI online™ platform.

Complete Transcript of Special Episode 3

Hi, this is Ivan Misner, Founder & Chief Visionary Officer of BNI. This is a Special Episode of the Official BNI Podcast.

I know many of you are struggling.

Just to give you an update, we have invested massive resources to transition BNI to an online platform (think of 9,500 Zoom accounts for all of our groups, along with the training, and staffing resources to create manuals and processes in just a matter of weeks). We have invested heavily to make sure you, our members can continue to benefit from being part of BNI.

Know that BNI has been impacted as well. To put this in perspective, we have had to lay off about a third of our global staff (hopefully on an interim basis). We hope to bring many of them back. ALL remaining BNI employees have taken an across the board 25% – 50% pay cut.

BNI is a face-to-face networking company. Our entire business was predicated on in-person meetings. We are trying to turn-on-a-dime to continue offering services to you, our members through BNI online™. Transparency is important and I believe that sharing this information with you is the right thing to do.

I also want to communicate another message. I truly believe we will prevail. You will prevail. We will prevail together. We will all get through this, and BNI intends to be right there with you.

A message will go out soon as to what we can do for the members AND still stay in business ourselves. Be patient and be understanding. We are all in this together. All of us.

In the meantime, I want to share with you some of the amazing stories that I have received regarding BNI online™. In a time of crisis, we are coming together to support one another, and I thought you might find some of these success stories to be inspiring. I know I have.

Before I start with the stories sent in, last week, let me start with a question that I was asked about BNI online™. I recently had a chapter President say to me that she was having difficulty getting one of her long-time “seasoned members” (I think she meant Baby Boomers) to use the platform – the BNI online™ platform. She said he barely used BNI Connect. What could she tell him to get him to participate?

I told her this. I HATE change. No, seriously, I hate change. The one thing I hate more than change, is failure. I really hate failure. He can change or he can fail. The choice is his. Tell him you’re there to help him through this if, he too, hates failure more than change. You’re there for him.

Change or failure.

I want to talk about the change that has taken place with BNI online™ and how it is leading to success for our members. Sit back for a while – I want to give you some good ones. Many of these have been edited down so I can cover more.

Mario Zambrano a member and friend of mine in Austin said: “I have been blown away by the speed BNI pivoted to online. Today we had our first meeting and it was dynamic and productive. I know how much it takes to make it work and I have never seen it done in one week. Bravo.”

[Now the amazing thing with this about comment this comment from Mario is that he is the Chief Technology Officer for a very successful technology company.]

Arumugam says, “I’m with a Titanium chapter of 95 members in India, we had 100% attendance at our 1st BNI online™ Chapter meeting. It was an awesome experience, positive contribution by all members. Moreover, we inducted a new member. Thank you BNI.”

Dr. Molina said, “All of my clinics are closed due to the quarantine. Amazingly, because of member referrals we received an order for 10,000 boxes! It is amazing how BNI connects people during a time when businesses are really down. “

Rick says, “I am in Thousand Oaks, CA. Today we had our first BNI online™ meeting. The team decided to forego the usual educational moment and use that time to have a couple of members inform the chapter how to navigate this crisis. They gave information on how to apply for government loans and how to deal with their mortgages. It was great to see all the members excited to help the businesses most affected by the lockdowns.”

[Thanks Rick, it is a great example of how BNI is more than just referrals. It is about relationships.]

Amit says “BNI online™ works as an anti-depressant and connects me with the world while we are locked up in our homes and shows a ray of hope that things will be normal again.”

Katrina in Australia says I found out today that we will be welcoming 2 NEW MEMBERS before our first online meeting tomorrow morning. Way to go BNI. I am so proud of us!!”

Phillip says, “We’ve been through the Great Flood of 2016 and now this mess. What surprised me was this…

“We had perfect attendance of 35 members! (We even had 3-4 visitors). I expected to hear about a lot about struggles, but instead I heard a lot of excitement. Small business owners are creative and nimble. Everyone was finding ways to keep their business moving forward.

“Who needs a bailout? People helping people and creative solutions are what makes small business great!”

Stanley says, “BNI online™ is the best development in BNI by far. It’s an awesome way of connecting. In a time of isolation, you now have a chance for your business – at no additional cost. Thank you, BNI.”

Heather from South Africa says, “We had our first BNI meeting online yesterday. It’s amazing how in this time of uncertainty we can still network and see all of our BNI friends. “

Elbert from the Philippines says, “We’ve been taking advantage of BNI online™ to ask for referral sources instead of referrals to clients, and then invite them as visitors into the chapter! At this morning’s meeting, we had 30 visitors. We are planting the seeds now, so that when the situation gets better, we’re ready to harvest and hit the ground running!”

Cincy says, “During this lock down period, it’s great that I am associated with this international organization. The online meetings are helping us to inspire and get inspired.”

Krishna from Kolkata says, “BNI online™ has become the light illuminating the lives of its members every single day, giving us hope, keeping us together, showing us the way to be in touch with our clients and friends. I’m a proud BNI member.”

Natalia says, “Our tireless director invited members of a group in Mexico and in Colombia for our second meeting online and showed us the real scope of BNI .”

Emmanuel from Tanzania says, “It is fantastic and convenient. You save travel time, breakfast charges, and the meeting time. In the future we should look at the possibility of combining online meetings and physical meetings.”

[Hmmm, Emmanuel, you may be right.]

Tommy says, “Shutters are being closed and countries are going under complete lockdown. But there’s a ray of hope with BNI. Chapter meetings, trainings, workshops, and member one-to-ones, everything is happening online. I am proud to be a part of this transition. We also see many members helping each other in this time of distress.”

Lawrence from Canada says, “We did Leadership Team training all online and had better attendance than ever. I loved it.”

Charlie from UK says, “I was extremely apprehensive about meeting online, but I have to say – what a meeting! Plenty of referrals were passed, everyone was positive, and it was emotional too. I want to thank BNI for the swift action during the outbreak. I truly believe that in times like these, BNI and the relationships built in the room, go a long way towards carrying us through darker times.”

Here’s my last story today: Runjuhn from India shared the story of Avinash, who couldn’t make the in-person meetings for BNI because he was watching his young daughter, Laishia, but with BNI online™ he was able to attend. He said, “BNI has helped change lives and BNI online™ is doing even more during these testing times. I’m grateful to be reunited with my BNI friends when I needed them most. Thank you BNI for everyone who has made this happen.”

[Runjuhn mentioned in her email to me that during a committee debrief he introduced us to his daughter who just woke up from sleep and was still able to see Dad around.]

These are amazing stories and they are only a fraction of what I received last week.

This, BNI members, is the way you turn a big ole batch of lemons into a vat of lemonade. We are all feeling the pinch right now. We will get through this and with BNI, we will be better positioned than most to move on when it’s over.

On a personal note, I want to give a big thank you to Graham Weihmiller (CEO of BNI). Graham saw this coming before I did. He was looking around a corner. He was moving on putting BNI online™ in place before anyone thought it was necessary. I also need to thank the full Global Support Team. We couldn’t have done it without them. I really look forward to bringing most of the full team back when possible.

Lastly, I want to thank the many Directors and Leadership Team members around the world who moved mountains to get the 9,500 groups to the online platform. You are all to be commended.

So, this brings this podcast to an end. Unless something changes, I intend to run a regular episode of the Official BNI Podcast next week. We recorded a few before the COVID virus shut things down and I’ve been holding them back to put out these special messages to you. Next week’s topic is a perfect one for these days – it is all about gratitude. As needed, I will record Special Episodes in the future.

For now, I’d love to hear how you’re making BNI online™ work. Please post it here in the comments section. I read every one.

You stay healthy, and we’ll help you stay connected.

This is Ivan Misner, Founder of BNI.

Thank you.

ShareTweetLinkedinWhatsappSaveSendFlipboard

Part of the series Special Episodes Posted in Online Networking Tagged with BNI online™

Comments

    Solutions-focused comments are welcome. Dr. Misner reads every comment personally.

    All comments are moderated before posting. Profanity, personal attacks, and sales pitches will be deleted.

  1. Yvonne Katamba says

    April 1, 2020 at 1:48 am

    Our first online meeting found me quarantined far away from home in Mumbai, Maharashtra, Morbe Village. It was very refreshing to Network with my BNI family!

    We were a full house , no substitutes, no late comers
    Very exciting.

    I wish to thank our Directors who worked swiftly to have this happen, just in time.

    Yvonne Katamba – BNI Sunrise , Kampala.

    Reply

  2. JACK G BOYD says

    April 1, 2020 at 7:06 pm

    I am a 14 year member of BNI Money Makers in St. Augustine, FL. Yesterday we had a virtual regional Round Table meeting. We had a record attendance of 108 members representing 25 chapters in North East Florida. By meeting virtually, no one had to drive to a single location or schedule the 90 minute meeting time in addition to the drive time. Consequently, members from all over the region attended, many of them never having met fellow chapter members in distant parts of north east Florida. The meeting was well run, productive and exciting. Kudos to Jamie Stein and Stefanie Robinson who organized and ran the meeting. BNI is, once again, showing the business world how it’s done. I remain a proud and grateful BNI member!

    Reply

  3. Ramesh Nair says

    April 1, 2020 at 11:15 pm

    I am Ramesh Nair, from Thampurans chapter,Thrissur, India. We deal with Wheelchair and Mobility Aids.
    Nobody expects an accident to happen, when you step out of your house. It happens and you become physically challenged.
    We say, you walk in a farm and then a coconut falls on your head.How sad… The best part of the story is that you pick up that coconut and make a coconut curry ,out of the fallen coconut. That needs loud applause.
    That’s what BNI online has done to us. You made the coconut curry. We are not left out. Thank you BNI

    Reply

  4. Rajan Verma says

    April 2, 2020 at 3:16 am

    I am 1 year + member to BNI India Delhi West Prolific. BNI online has been a saviour for me at the time of complete lockdown. The first 2-3 days were loom and no business, no calls, no meetings and I was getting into depression, irritated and causing lot of mess for people around. However, then our BNI online came and we had our first online meeting, then a few sessions from our ED to use BNI online and few online trainings… which gave me a spark. I have been having atleast one 1-2-1 within chapter of 100 people and one 1-2-1 with cross chapter for past 7 days… this has given a meaningful time spending and will surely result in business for fellow members who can still serve, likes of astrology service etc. I am feeling happy to have BNI and BNI Online now with us in this difficult time. Wishing all to stay safe and healthy and wishing to see all at the end of this.

    Reply

    • Someshwer Cherukure says

      April 7, 2020 at 3:16 am

      Bingo, Mr. Verma,

      Well said ji, Im a new member here at Hyderabad West, BUSINET, joined on the 4th of March 2020. Into Events & Weddings and I know for sure that Events are going to go down and though Weddings are going to happen, it is going to be vert Suttle n Lowdown. SO, in this gloomy sunrise, BNI ONLINE is a true stand-alone buddy. Gave us all a lot of pats, lot of strength, lot of hope and lot of pull for the future. NIce reading your comments Sir.

      Stay home, stay alive

      Reply

  5. Joann Babiak says

    April 2, 2020 at 3:52 pm

    I am a very grateful new member of BNI Network 54, located in Larkspur, Marin County, California. Your astute observation that we are all of us in this together is demonstrated by the care of members in my chapter. They are, without exception, welcoming, encouraging, and selflessly give of their time and expertise without being actually called upon to do so. BNI online adds structure to a world that is at present filled with uncertainty. The online connection helps keep us productive, informed and able to create new alliances. All of us realize that we are better together. Thank you for the opportunity to normalize our interactions during this challenging time.

    Reply

  6. Roberta O’Dea says

    April 2, 2020 at 5:18 pm

    We are getting more visitors than ever since taking our meeting online and we continue to exceed our KPI’s and their is much more emphasis on doing this together. Thanks BNI and thanks Business By The Sea in Williamstown Melbourne Australia

    Reply

  7. Vijaykumar says

    April 2, 2020 at 9:41 pm

    Hi guys
    It is really amazing transformation by BNI
    QUICKLY TURNING INTO ON LINE.
    I WAS ABOUT LEAVE BNI, But our new LT team
    changed this all
    Am pharma professional worked in Indian and international market for 2 decades & last 10 years in my Indian n International business
    And less than a year IN BNI, VICTORY, MUMBAI chapter. Thanks to “BNI SWIFT”
    ON LINE TRANSFORMATION
    AM SURE MANY MEMBERS WILL GAIN IN THESE TRYING TIMES INCLUDING US

    Reply

    • Someshwer Cherukure says

      April 7, 2020 at 3:12 am

      Hey Vijay – well said Sir. We have one more member, who just was about to leave but all of have coaxed him to think again. and He did stay back. Great beginnings, great tidings end up Great

      Love you

      Rgds
      9398798052

      Reply

  8. Edson M . de Moraes says

    April 3, 2020 at 7:28 am

    BNI Esta de Parabens pelo trabalho de todos os Diretores e colaboradores, por terem feito esse migração em um tempo muito curto para BNI ONLINE.
    Reuniões estão sendo muito produtivas, fazendo as equipes do trabalho estarem mais unidas e participativas com refenrencias e ideais para passarmos por esse processo mais fortes e saiar com prosperidade dele.
    Abraços

    Reply

  9. Charissa says

    April 3, 2020 at 10:35 am

    These stories were so fun to hear! I love feeling connected to BNI on a Global scale. How cool is it that through a time when we are all meant to be “farther” apart, we are actually coming together and getting closer. What a gift!

    Reply

  10. Tonya Acha says

    April 3, 2020 at 11:55 am

    Ivan, thank you and the entire Global Support Team so much for your vision and leadership. This episode was perfect for me today. I am an Area director and have been supporting chapters for 11 years. These past few weeks have been both challenging AND rewarding however today I had a bit of a struggle keeping myself motivated and felt I was climbing an uphill battle. This podcast and the testimonials are just what I needed to get my mindset back on track and I am confident it will also support the chapters in my area that have been feeling some pushback on BNI Online. Thank you for all you do!

    Reply

  11. Dhammika Jayasuriya says

    April 4, 2020 at 9:55 pm

    Am DD from Tycoons chapter of Srilanka the zoom initiative is excellent all initiators from SL and your Ceo must be commended, as a team our LT and some Senior persons are initiating many strategies to help each other, we had a very high profile Visitor this week , and as a team our chapter is Determined to bounce back with Entrepreneurship.

    Reply

  12. Kraig says

    April 5, 2020 at 3:26 pm

    Ivan, thank you so much for the special podcasts. I just joined BNI 3 weeks before things went into shut down. Thankfully I was able to have 6 121’s with members already and have been doing phone call 121’s each week with a member or two to get to know my chapter members. It is comforting to be around other business owners and mangers that are feeling the pinch and are encouraging each other and really caring about how everyone is doing. It gives me that extra kick to keep my head up and take this time to plan my company’s growth to exceed my expectations now that I have a support of many other BNI members. I am thankful that we have the online BNI meetings every week and we are even having a happy hour online meeting every week so we can all visit and encourage one another and just relax and be able to visit. One of the best things that has come out of this shut down is that it has allowed for more time to think and make plans and strategize to progress my business and be ready when gates open to return back to full work force. I feel like a horse at the starting gates ready to break free and charge in making my business grow unshakably.

    Reply

    • Someshwer Cherukure says

      April 7, 2020 at 3:10 am

      Absolutely Kraig, Im also as new as you. Joined on the 4th of April 2020. Welcome to the new world, Sir. Love to family — STAY HOME, STAY ALIVE

      Reply

  13. Someshwer Cherukure says

    April 7, 2020 at 3:08 am

    Dr. Misner – Good Afternoon (IST)

    It was a stupendous effort to have taken-off BNI onto the online platform ZOOM and make it all look so easy. Great job Sir. I am a new member here in India, BUSINET, Hyderabad West and I am into Events & Weddings. Trust me, this phase looks bad but if you loo in the inside, things are looking pretty good, rosy, shaped-up, brighter, more opportunities, more time for different things, more time for one’s family, more time for all those nick-nacks, which have been under the carpet for a long time. Now, the question is – how long? This is what everyone wants to know, but instead, what I would say is – Why not? Let it BE, let’s go on like this as long as it tests our patience – BNI-ites do not get tired at all. Wanted to repear it after you – “WE ARE ALL IN THIS TOGETHER”, Yes sir, we are……

    But the best part is, The World over, things are changing so rapidly and so immaculately that we are all peeking around for new businesses, new opportunities (like I mentioned above), new relationships, new horizons, new peek-a-boos…..they are all lying on the floor waiting to be taken to new heights. Let’s do it, do it together. Let BNI, be that guiding factor for all of us to shine brightly ever after

    Love you Sir

    Brave of you…..

    Someshwer Cherukure
    Hyderabad, India
    +91.93987.98052
    [email protected]

    Reply

  14. Amanda Goff says

    April 15, 2020 at 4:41 pm

    BNI is keeping me connected with my support system and helping me also feel a little more normal. We feel safe in our BNI family to vent, but also problem solve.

    Reply

  15. Mark Bush says

    May 26, 2020 at 6:52 pm

    Having been a member of BNI and a Director consultant these past 17 years, it never ceases to amaze me just how powerful and GIVING this wonderful organization truly is! The online platform has really SKYROCKETED BNI to heights we could have not even imagined! Thank you BNI and thank you Invan!

    Reply

  16. Neta Wenrick says

    May 29, 2020 at 12:35 pm

    Thank you Dr. Misner and BNI for keeping us going during this challenging time.
    Our chapter continues to have 100% attendance during the online meetings and have added a new members. Many visitors who have not been able to attend in the past, are now attending and seeing the value of BNI, as we continue to pass referrals and share TYFCB. I am able to visit chapter in my area and meet new potential referrals partners, that would not be able to visit otherwise. I am blessed to be a BNI member for nearly 14 years.

    Reply

  17. Raju Rambatla says

    July 17, 2021 at 4:30 am

    I am Raju Rambatla , BNI Emperor, Dubai, chapter just launched Couple of months ago, prior to which the team
    Of 15 enthusiasts were calling each other CGM
    I am a new kid on the BNI Block
    With stage 3 cancer and expecting to maintain 100 percent attendance , ideally speaking: soon after an intense session of chemo and radiation, the next early
    Morning OnLINE networking is an amazing cake walk with 25 members

    With the Delta Variant mutations, and new strains of Covid pandemic, with the Global vaccine mismanagement and poorly handled distribution chaos, on line networking is becoming the new norm from kindergartens to global business developments

    We need to put full emphasis and perhaps Create a full style on line platform
    With occasional face to face guarded networking

    BNI has risen to this predicted challenge by Ivan Misner in2018 in a wonderful manner

    Bravo
    Raju
    Freight Forwarding and logistics
    Dubai
    [email protected]
    Cell: 971551039948

    Reply
